original_hypothesis.title: ETH_RUNE transferTo Uses tx.origin Enabling Token Theft via Any Intermediary Contract
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ETH_RUNE.transferTo(recipient, amount) uses tx.origin instead of msg.sender to determine the source of funds. Any contract in the call chain can call transferTo to transfer tokens from the original transaction signer without their approval. A malicious contract could call transferTo(attacker, victim_full_balance) during any interaction with the victim, draining all their ETH_RUNE.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The code explicitly uses tx.origin and even documents the risk in comments. While this is a known design choice for upgrade compatibility, it creates a severe vulnerability that could be exploited by any contract the user interacts with.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Protocol docs state ETH_RUNE is deprecated and out of scope. However, the contract is in the auditable list and the code defect is real.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _transfer(tx.origin, recipient, amount) - uses tx.origin instead of msg.sender

preconditions: Victim interacts with any contract (even unrelated to RUNE)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _transfer(tx.origin, recipient, amount) bypasses approval system entirely
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Deploy malicious contract; victim interacts with it for any reason; drain all RUNE
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker captures victim's entire ETH_RUNE balance

economic_questions: How many users still hold ETH_RUNE?
What is the total value of ETH_RUNE in circulation?
Has ownership been renounced as documented?
validation_requirements: Deploy a contract that calls transferTo in its fallback/function
Have a test user interact with it while holding ETH_RUNE
Verify full balance is transferred to attacker

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Gas for deploying malicious contract (~0.01 ETH)

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: DEX liquidity to sell stolen RUNE
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Could depress RUNE price if large amounts sold

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Any ETH_RUNE holder who interacts with a malicious contract

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm transferTo bypasses all approval checks

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
